Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard Hire to Retire Process Logic The typical sequence would be Post a job advertisement Screen candidates Conduct interviews Make a hiring decision Onboard the employee if hired Add to payroll Close the case Key logical constraints You must post a job ad before screening candidates You must screen candidates before conducting interviews or at least before making a decision You must conduct interviews before making a hiring decision You must make a hiring decision before onboarding You should onboard before adding to payroll Closing the case should be the final step

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 Analysis Let me trace the order Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close So the partial order is Post must come before Screen Screen must come before both Decide and Interview Decide must come before Onboard Onboard must come before Payroll Payroll must come before Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The issue here is that Screen Decide and Screen Interview but there is no ordering between Decide and Interview This means After screening you could make a hiring decision before conducting interviews Or you could conduct interviews and then make a decision

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The anomaly Making a hiring decision can occur before conducting interviews This is a significant anomaly because you typically need to interview candidates before making a hiring decision

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Wait let me re read Screen Decide means Screen must come before Decide Screen Interview means Screen must come before Interview But there is no edge between Decide and Interview So after Screen both Decide and Interview can happen but their relative order is not specified

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 Analysis Let me trace the order Post Screen Post Interview Interview Decide Decide loop onboarding loop onboarding xor payroll xor payroll Close The partial order Post must come before Screen Post must come before Interview Interview must come before Decide Decide must come before loop onboarding loop onboarding must come before xor payroll xor payroll must come before Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies in Model 2 Post Screen and Post Interview but no edge between Screen and Interview This means after posting the job ad screening and interviewing can happen in any order or even in parallel This is an anomaly because you should screen candidates before conducting interviews

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: loop onboarding means onboarding can be repeated or skipped entirely While onboarding might have some iterative elements typically onboarding an employee once is sufficient The loop allows for repeated onboarding of the same employee which does not make logical sense

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: xor payroll means you can either add the employee to payroll or skip it entirely This is a significant anomaly because adding a new employee to payroll is a mandatory step in the hire to retire process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 has more anomalies and some are more severe Skipping payroll is a fundamental violation The loop on onboarding does not make practical sense The lack of ordering between screening and interviewing is also problematic

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Actually let me reconsider Model 1 In a partial order all nodes must eventually happen The question is just about ordering So Interview will happen but it could happen after Decide

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Model 1 has fewer anomalies and its anomalies are less severe ordering issue versus skipping mandatory steps The overall flow structure is more correct All mandatory steps are mandatory no optional payroll

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 is closer to the normative process The main fix needed is adding an edge from Interview to Decide Model 2 needs adding edge from Screen to Interview removing the loop on onboarding and removing the xor on payroll making it mandatory
